hero.sub: The intelligent fishing app that understands your catches. AI-powered predictions, tide tracking and community — all in one app.

features.title: Everything you need to fish smarter
features.1_desc: Take a photo — the AI identifies the species, logs measurements and calculates your Strike Score automatically.
features.2_desc: Solunar phases, tide calendars, wind forecasts and barometric pressure trends for the perfect fishing moment.
features.3_desc: The app analyses your catches and recommends the optimal bait — based on season, water body and weather.
features.4_desc: Verified catches via GPS & photo. Transparent community rating — fair and tamper-proof.
features.5_desc: Fishing licences for 14 countries right in the app. Buy, store and show them — no paperwork needed.
features.6_desc: Crews, tournaments, live chat and professional fishing coaches for your next session.

how.label: How it works
how.title: Three steps to the perfect strike
how.1_desc: Take a photo, confirm the species, enter measurements. In under 60 seconds your catch is documented — including weather, GPS and bait.
how.2_desc: After each catch your personal skill profile is refined. The AI analyses patterns in your successes and failures.
how.3_desc: Personalised Strike Score forecasts: when does which fish bite, at which spot, with which bait? StrikeAhead knows.

testimonials.label: Testimonials
testimonials.title: What anglers say
testimonials.1_text: Since I started using StrikeAhead, my catch rate has doubled. The AI predictions are uncannily accurate — my best pike came exactly in the window the app recommended.
testimonials.2_text: The licence manager alone is worth the subscription. I fish in 5 different countries and finally have all my permits in one place. Plus the community — highly recommended!
testimonials.3_text: The Trust Score has made tournaments fairer. No more fake catches — everyone can see instantly if a catch is GPS-verified. Finally an app that takes sport anglers seriously.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Find your plan
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Upgrade anytime. Cancel monthly.

devices.label: Platform
devices.title: Always with you — on every device
devices.subtitle: One app, three platforms. Your catches are always in sync — whether you're at the lake or at your desk.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes! The Free plan is free forever. You can log up to 10 catches per month, use AI species recognition and read the community feed. Upgrade anytime, cancel monthly.
faq.q2: Which platforms is the app available on?
faq.a2: StrikeAhead runs as a web app in any browser (desktop & mobile), as a native iOS app on the App Store and as an Android app on Google Play. All platforms are fully synchronised.
faq.q3: How accurate are the AI catch predictions?
faq.a3: Prediction accuracy improves with every logged catch. Our model combines your personal catch history with weather data, solunar phases, tide calendars and community data. After about 20 catches, predictions are significantly personalised.
faq.q4: Which countries does the Licence Manager support?
faq.a4: Currently Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Netherlands, Belgium, France, Spain, Portugal, Italy, Croatia, Poland, Denmark, Sweden and Norway. More countries are being added.
faq.q5: Are my GPS data and fishing spots shared?
faq.a5: Never without your permission. Every catch is private by default. You decide what to share with the community. GPS coordinates are encrypted and never passed to third parties.
faq.q6: Can I use StrikeAhead as a fishing coach?
faq.a6: Yes! With the Legend plan you can create your coaching profile, take on students, manage bookings and even host your own tournaments.

final_cta.title: Your next catch is waiting.
final_cta.sub: Get started now — on iOS, Android or right in your browser. No credit card needed for the Free plan.

angelschule.title: Fishing School
angelschule.subtitle: Knowledge & Learning

equipment_beginner.title: Fishing Equipment for Beginners
equipment_beginner.body:
**Your starter kit**

You don't need much to get going: an all-round rod (2.40–2.70 m), a matching spinning reel (size 3000–4000), plus monofilament line (0.25–0.30 mm) or braided line (0.15 mm) for better sensitivity.

**Accessories**

A small box with hooks in different sizes (6–12), sinkers, swivels, floats, and pliers is plenty for your first trips. Don't forget: landing net, measuring tape, hook remover, and your fishing license.

first_license.title: Getting Your First Fishing License
first_license.body:
**The fishing license in Germany**

In most German states you need a Fischereischein, which requires passing an exam covering biology, water science, gear, law, and animal protection — plus a practical test on rigging.

**On site**

You also need a permit card (guest, weekly, or yearly) for the specific water you fish. Get it from the club, tackle shop, or online. Without both, every fishing day is illegal — checks are frequent.

knots_beginner.title: Knots for Beginners
knots_beginner.body:
**The Clinch Knot**

The classic beginner knot: pass the line through the hook eye, wrap 5–6 times around the main line, then feed the tag end through the small loop at the eye and back through the large loop. Wet it and pull tight.

**The Loop Knot**

For attaching leaders: form a simple overhand loop, wrap twice, pull the end through, and tighten. The loop lets you swap leaders fast — perfect for changing conditions.

spinning_basics.title: Spin Fishing — Basic Technique
spinning_basics.body:
**What is spin fishing?**

Active fishing with artificial lures: you cast a spinner, crankbait, or soft plastic and retrieve it purposefully. By varying retrieve speed and adding pauses, you mimic wounded baitfish.

**Typical target species**

Pike, perch, zander, and trout respond well to lures. Dawn and dusk are prime. Fan-cast the area systematically — your first cast should land where a fish might be holding, not where it's comfortable.

bottom_fishing.title: Bottom Fishing
bottom_fishing.body:
**The bottom rig**

In bottom fishing the bait sits on the waterbed. Classic setup: a running lead above a swivel, followed by a 50–80 cm leader with a hook. The fish feels no resistance when it bites.

**Patience pays off**

Ideal for carp, bream, tench, and eel. Baits: corn, boilies, lobworms, or maggots. Location matters: weed edges, deep holes, and tributary mouths all promise fish.

fly_fishing_intro.title: Fly Fishing — Introduction
fly_fishing_intro.body:
**The essence of fly fishing**

Instead of casting the lure, you cast the line — the artificial fly drifts almost weightlessly at the tippet's end. The rhythm of forward and back casts loads the rod until the line rolls out elegantly.

**First steps**

An AFTM 5/6 combo suits trout and grayling. Start on still water without wind, distances of 8–10 meters. Precision beats distance — a fly 20 cm from a fish beats one 30 meters away.

palomar_knot.title: The Palomar Knot
palomar_knot.body:
**Why the Palomar?**

Easy to tie and extremely strong — the Palomar retains up to 95% of line strength. Especially popular for braided lines where many knots slip.

**Step by step**

Double the line, loop it through the eye. Tie a loose overhand knot with the double loop. Pass the loop over the hook. Wet, pull slowly tight, trim tag to 2 mm.

fg_knot.title: FG Knot (Braid-Fluoro)
fg_knot.body:
**The FG knot for braid and fluorocarbon**

The FG links mainline and shock leader almost invisibly and runs smoothly through guides. Lets you pair thin braid with a stronger leader.

**Practice pays**

Trickier than other knots but extremely reliable once learned. Tip: keep the fluorocarbon under tension and wrap the braid around it in alternating directions — at least 20 wraps. Secure with two half-hitches.

rod_guide.title: Rod Guide: Choosing the Right One
rod_guide.body:
**Choosing by application**

Length determines casting distance and control: 2.10 m for stream trout, 2.70 m all-round, 3.60 m+ for carp and feeder. The casting weight (WG) should match your lure.

**Action**

Fast (tip action) = sensitive bite detection, stiff. Moderate (semi-parabolic) = universal, forgiving. Slow (parabolic) = soft, casts light baits. Beginners should pick moderate.

reel_setup.title: Setting Up Your Reel
reel_setup.body:
**Mounting the reel**

Screw the reel to the rod, open the bail, thread the line through all guides, and secure it to the spool with an arbor knot. Never overfill the spool — 2 mm clearance prevents wind knots.

**Setting the drag**

The drag should slip at about 25–30% of line strength. Rule of thumb: pulling the line should release line evenly before it breaks. Check before every fight.

catch_release.title: Catch & Release Done Right
catch_release.body:
**Fish-friendly landing**

Use a rubberized landing net — it protects the slime coat. Play the fish briskly so it doesn't exhaust. Hold horizontally, never vertically by the jaw — that damages the spine.

**Back in the water**

Unhook in the water if possible. If the hook is deep: cut the leader; the hook will rust out. Hold the fish underwater until it swims off under its own power. Never throw it.

clean_fishing.title: Trash-Free Fishing
clean_fishing.body:
**A clean swim**

Every bit of old line, lead, and discarded hook endangers birds and fish. Always carry a small trash bag — yours and others'. Leave the spot cleaner than you found it.

**Lead-free alternatives**

Lead is toxic to waterfowl. Choose tungsten, steel, or stone. Same for bait: biodegradable options and reusable boxes cut plastic waste. Small gestures, big impact.

moon_phases.title: Moon Phases in Fishing
moon_phases.body:
**New moon and full moon**

Strong tides and elevated activity, especially among predators in coastal waters. The twilight hours on these days can be extraordinary.

**In freshwater**

The moon affects inland waters too — more subtly. Many experienced anglers swear by the 3 days before and after a full moon. Keep a log and correlate your catches with moon phase.

barometer_activity.title: Barometric Pressure and Fish Activity
barometer_activity.body:
**Pressure and bite behavior**

Falling pressure (usually before weather changes) activates many fish — they feed before the storm. High, stable pressure often means calm conditions and slow activity.

**In practice**

Watch 12–24 hours ahead of your trip. Rapid changes (> 3 hPa / 3h) are prime time. Combine with moon phase and water temperature for sharper forecasts.

angelschule.article_reading_weather_body:
Air pressure significantly influences fish behavior.

Rising Pressure (High Pressure):
• Fish become more active
• Ideal fishing conditions after pressure rises
• Fish come from deeper waters

Falling Pressure (Low Pressure Front):
• Fish retreat to deeper layers
• Often good catches just before the front
• Usually slow after the front passes

Stable Pressure: Consistent activity, predictable.
